userStories.title: KB CLI commands — koda kb add/search/list
userStories.description: Create `apps/cli/src/commands/kb.ts` with three Commander.js sub-commands under the `kb` parent command: `koda kb search`, `koda kb list`, and `koda kb add`. Register `kbCommand(program)` in `apps/cli/src/index.ts`. Each command must follow the standard CLI pattern: `resolveAuth({})` → `configureClient()` → generated service call → `unwrap()` → formatted output. The `search` command renders a verdict line plus ranked results with score labels (HIGH/MED/LOW), confidence score, ticket ref, type, status, and labels. The `list` command renders a table with columns ID, Source, Created. The `add` command reads a file from disk and POSTs it as a manual document, printing a success confirmation. All three must support `--json` for machine-readable output and use `handleApiError(err)` for error handling. Missing required flags exit with code 3; missing auth exits with code 2. Co-locate unit tests in `apps/cli/src/commands/kb.spec.ts` that mock the generated service and assert output shape and exit codes.
acceptanceCriteria: koda kb search --project koda --query 'auth error' exits 0 and prints verdict + ranked results
koda kb search --json outputs raw JSON matching API response shape
koda kb list --project koda exits 0 and prints document table with ID, Source, Created columns
koda kb add --project koda --file ./README.md exits 0 and prints success confirmation with file name and doc count
Missing --project or --query causes exit code 3 (Commander required option enforcement)
Missing auth config (no apiKey or apiUrl) causes exit code 2
API errors are handled via handleApiError(err)
kbCommand(program) is registered in apps/cli/src/index.ts after existing command registrations
Unit tests in kb.spec.ts cover search verdict output, list table format, add confirmation, and auth-missing path
bun run test passes in apps/cli

userStories.title: Agent auto-pickup API endpoint — GET /api/agents/:slug/pickup
userStories.description: Add `suggestTicket(agentSlug: string, projectSlug: string)` to `AgentsService` and a corresponding `GET :slug/pickup?project=<projectSlug>` endpoint to `AgentsController`. The algorithm loads the agent with its capabilities, queries all VERIFIED unassigned tickets in the project (status='VERIFIED', assignedToAgentId=null, assignedToUserId=null, deletedAt=null), scores each ticket by the count of capability names that appear in the ticket's label names, sorts descending by score then priority order (CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW), and returns the top candidate with `{ ticket, matchScore, matchedCapabilities }` or null if no candidates exist. The endpoint returns `JsonResponse.Ok(result)` with a 404 `AppException` if the agent slug is not found and a 400 `AppException` if the `project` query param is missing. Add supertest E2E coverage and unit tests in `agents.service.spec.ts` covering: highest-scored ticket selected, null when no VERIFIED unassigned tickets, 404 for unknown agent, score-0 returns highest-priority ticket as fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/agents/:slug/pickup?project=koda returns { ret: 0, data: { ticket, matchScore, matchedCapabilities } } for a matching agent with candidates
Response data is null (ret still 0) when no VERIFIED unassigned tickets exist in the project
Only VERIFIED tickets with both assignedToAgentId=null and assignedToUserId=null are considered candidates
Ties in score are broken by priority order: CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW
Returns 404 AppException if agent slug is not found
Returns 400 AppException if project query param is missing
Endpoint is decorated with Swagger @ApiOperation, @ApiQuery, @ApiResponse decorators
Unit tests cover happy path, no-match null, unknown agent 404, and score-0 priority fallback
Supertest E2E test covers the pickup endpoint
bun run test passes in apps/api

userStories.title: Auto-assign config on Project + koda agent pickup CLI command
userStories.description: Three coordinated changes: (1) API schema — add `autoAssign String @default("OFF")` to the Project model in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`, run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_auto_assign`, add `AutoAssignMode` const enum to `apps/api/src/common/enums.ts`, add optional `@IsIn(['OFF','SUGGEST','AUTO'])` `autoAssign` field to `create-project.dto.ts` and `update-project.dto.ts`, and expose `autoAssign` in project service responses. (2) API regeneration — run `bun run generate` from monorepo root so the CLI generated client reflects the updated Project type and new pickup endpoint. (3) CLI extension — add `pickup` sub-command to the existing `koda agent` command in `apps/cli/src/commands/agent.ts`: `koda agent pickup --project <slug> [--json]`. The pickup command calls the `GET /api/agents/:slug/pickup` endpoint using the current agent's slug (from auth config), formats a human-readable suggestion or 'no match' message, and supports `--json` for raw output. Unit tests cover the DTO validation (invalid autoAssign → 400), project create default, project update persistence, and CLI pickup command output.
acceptanceCriteria: autoAssign field added to Prisma Project model with @default("OFF")
Migration created and applied without errors via prisma migrate dev
AutoAssignMode const enum (OFF, SUGGEST, AUTO) added to apps/api/src/common/enums.ts
create-project.dto.ts and update-project.dto.ts accept optional autoAssign field validated as one of OFF|SUGGEST|AUTO
Invalid autoAssign value (e.g. 'INVALID') returns 400 validation error
autoAssign is returned in project list and show API responses
Creating a project without autoAssign results in autoAssign='OFF' in the response
koda agent pickup --project koda exits 0 and prints suggested ticket details or 'No suitable tickets found for pickup.'
koda agent pickup --json outputs raw JSON from the pickup endpoint
bun run test passes in both apps/api and apps/cli

analysis:
## Codebase Analysis

### Entry Point Correction
The spec references `apps/cli/src/main.ts` but the actual CLI entry point is `apps/cli/src/index.ts` (152 lines). All story implementations must register commands in `apps/cli/src/index.ts`, not `main.ts`.

### Generated Client
The `apps/cli/src/generated/` directory does NOT exist yet. It is produced by `bun run generate:cli` from the monorepo root. Implementation agents for US-001 and US-003 CLI work must first read `openapi.json` at the monorepo root to identify the correct service class name(s) for KB and pickup endpoints. The spec names `KnowledgeBaseService` — confirm the actual class name after generation.

### CLI Command Pattern (from `label.ts` and `agent.ts`)
All commands follow: `resolveAuth({})` → check `auth.apiKey && auth.apiUrl` → `configureClient(auth.apiUrl, auth.apiKey)` → call generated service → `unwrap(response)` → format output. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=API error, 2=auth (401/403), 3=validation (400), 4=not found (404). `handleApiError(err)` covers API errors. `success()`, `error()`, `table()` from `utils/output.ts` for output. `--json` flag routes to `console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2))`.

### CLI Output Utilities
`apps/cli/src/utils/output.ts` exports `output()`, `table(headers, rows)`, `success(msg)`, `error(msg)`, `warn(msg)`. Use `success()` for add confirmations, `table()` for list output, custom format for search results with verdict.

### API: agents.service.ts
Currently 283 lines. Uses `@Inject('PrismaService')` pattern with `(this.prisma as any).client` to access Prisma client. `suggestTicket()` must follow the same PrismaService injection pattern. Throws `AppException` subclasses — use `NotFoundAppException` for unknown agent slug, standard `AppException` with HTTP 400 for missing query param.

### API: agents.controller.ts
144 lines. All endpoints return `JsonResponse.Ok(data)`. Uses `@ApiOperation`, `@ApiResponse`, `@ApiBearerAuth` Swagger decorators consistently. The new `GET :slug/pickup` endpoint should follow the same Swagger decoration pattern and accept both JWT and API key auth (use existing `CombinedAuthGuard`).

### enums.ts
56 lines with 6 existing const enums. `AutoAssignMode` must be added as a 7th following the exact same pattern: `export const AutoAssignMode = { OFF: 'OFF', SUGGEST: 'SUGGEST', AUTO: 'AUTO' } as const; export type AutoAssignMode = typeof AutoAssignMode[keyof typeof AutoAssignMode];`

### schema.prisma
175 lines. Project model currently has: id, name, slug, key, description, gitRemoteUrl, autoIndexOnClose, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt. Add `autoAssign String @default("OFF")` before closing brace. SQLite migration via `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_auto_assign`.

### Project DTOs
`create-project.dto.ts` (64 lines) uses class-validator with i18n keys. `autoAssign` should be `@IsOptional()`, `@IsString()`, `@IsIn(['OFF', 'SUGGEST', 'AUTO'])` following the same i18n error key pattern as existing fields.

### Dependency Order
US-001 (KB CLI) and US-002 (pickup API) are independent and can run in parallel. US-003 depends on US-002 being deployed/testable and also extends the CLI agent command from US-001's pattern.

### Test Patterns
CLI: Jest unit tests mock generated services and `resolveAuth`. API: Jest unit tests in `*.service.spec.ts` mock PrismaService. E2E tests use supertest against real NestJS app. Every new API endpoint MUST have supertest E2E coverage per project rules.
